loading_sequence: Load objective_profiles.json, sleeves.json, budget_policies.json, monitor_profiles.json, and executor_profiles.json in that order.
Materialize the frozen Dexter and Mew-X source-pin registry.
Apply plan_request_id, optional objective_profile_id, and portfolio_budget_id inputs.
Cross-validate references and emit one immutable resolved_planning_context.
invariants: Missing files fail closed.
Duplicate ids fail closed.
Pinned commits must match the frozen source registry before planning continues.
Runtime overrides may not mutate preferred source, sleeve enablement semantics, budget policy, monitor profile, or executor pin after load.

steps: If objective_profile_id is absent, substitute mixed_default.
Reject unknown explicit objective_profile_id values.
Require exactly one preferred_source.
Require single_sleeve or portfolio_split as the route_mode.
Require Dexter as the preferred source for portfolio_split templates.
Require containment-first explicitness for any Mew-X-preferred profile.
invariants: Omitted intent defaults to Dexter.
Explicit unknown intent is rejected rather than silently coerced.
Portfolio split is separate-sleeve only.
No multi-source sleeve or in-run fusion is permitted.

steps: Derive ordered candidate sleeves from the resolved objective profile.
For single_sleeve use only primary_sleeve_id.
For portfolio_split append overlay_sleeve_ids in declaration order.
Require every selected sleeve to be enabled and source-matched.
Reject the full request if any explicitly required sleeve is unavailable.
invariants: No silent fallback from explicit Mew-X intent to Dexter.
One emitted plan binds one sleeve and one source.
Overlay sleeves remain explicit and separate.

steps: Resolve budget_policy_id.
Resolve portfolio_budget_id, defaulting to main.
Derive one sleeve-scoped budget binding per selected sleeve.
Require explicit cap references for any nonzero Mew-X allocation.
Reject aggregate sleeve shares above 100%.
invariants: All budget declarations are sleeve-scoped.
Any Mew-X share is explicit and never implicit.
Numeric caps remain deferred, but cap references are fixed now.
No adaptive rebalance is implied after start.

steps: Build selected sleeves and budget bindings from the validated planning context.
Allocate one plan_batch_id.
Derive one immutable execution_plan per selected sleeve.
Populate route, sleeve_binding, budget_binding, monitor_binding, executor_binding, source_pin, and invariants.
Set initial normalized status to planned.
Persist or publish the full batch atomically.
invariants: One plan binds one sleeve and one source.
Route, sleeve, budget, monitor, and executor bindings are immutable after planned.
Source pins are copied into the plan rather than looked up dynamically later.
Either every plan in the batch is emitted or none are emitted.

steps: Call prepare(plan) for each plan in emitted order.
If any prepare fails, stop all prepared handles and fail the batch before start.
If all prepare calls succeed, transition plans to starting.
Call start(handle) in emitted order.
Accept only normalized running, quarantined, stopping, stopped, or failed transitions afterward.
invariants: Prepare is the all-or-nothing boundary for split batches.
No planner-side threshold or trust-logic rewriting occurs during dispatch.
No mid-trade cross-source handoff is allowed.
Sleeve-local failure does not mutate another plan's source binding.

rules: Planning-time validation failures occur before planned.
Failed never triggers automatic reroute to the other source.
Quarantined remains sleeve-scoped unless global halt escalates.
Mew-X inactivity_timeout is source-native behavior, not an automatic incident by itself.

invariants: No partial plan batch is emitted if any pre-emit validation fails.
No silent downgrade from explicit Mew-X intent to Dexter default is allowed.
No partial batch start occurs after a failed prepare.
Terminal failure is represented as failed rather than mutable route rebinding.

executor_boundary_spec: runtime auth or secret injection mechanics

why: Config loading, validation, route resolution, budget binding, plan emission, and dispatch order are now fixed strongly enough to map directly into modules and functions.
Monitor thresholds can attach to the fixed plan registry and normalized lifecycle without blocking code-structure design.
Executor transport remains bounded behind the already fixed prepare/start/status/stop/snapshot surface.

residual_and_caveats.replay_scope: Current zero-gap evidence is scoped to close-summary and position_closed realized-return fidelity rather than full execution-path equivalence.

dexter_metric_scope_gap.why_it_matters: A stronger claim about path-sensitive retention still requires a separate lane.

residual_and_caveats.candidate_surface_gap: Mew-X candidate_precision_proxy remains unavailable because the frozen export surface does not expose a comparable creator_candidate path.
residual_and_caveats.concrete_spec_boundary: Planner-side config loading, validation, plan emission, and dispatch order are now fixed, while numeric monitor thresholds, retry values, heartbeat values, and executor transport remain later defaults.

why: The fixed interface contract is now concrete enough to freeze deterministic planner-side load, resolve, bind, emit, and dispatch order.
Dexter remains the default execution backbone while Mew-X remains bounded to explicit containment-first sleeves and overlays only.
That is sufficient to move directly into a code-level module and function spec without reopening comparison work.
